title: Do You Love Scary Dinosaurs? J.A. Bayona’s 'Jurassic World: Fallen Kingdom' Has Scary Dinosaurs
format: Sean Fennessey talks to J.A. Bayona about directing 'Jurassic World: Fallen Kingdom': why he turned down the first film, the gothic-mansion second half, animatronics over green screen, and designing the Indoraptor.

films.title: Jurassic World: Fallen Kingdom
films.note: The subject. Bayona took the reins from Colin Trevorrow, pulling the dinosaurs off the island and into a gothic-mansion second half he shot like a haunted-house movie.

films.title: Jurassic World
films.note: Trevorrow's revival, the fifth-highest grosser at the time. Frank Marshall offered it to Bayona first, but with no script and a six-month turnaround he passed; they came back to him with the sequel.

films.title: Jurassic Park
films.note: Spielberg's 1993 original. Bayona's favorite scenes as a kid were the first T-Rex reveal and the raptors stalking the kitchen.

films.title: The Orphanage
films.note: Bayona's debut. Fennessey hears its quiet, lurking mood echoed in Hereditary.

films.title: A Monster Calls
films.note: Bayona's tree-monster film, built with CGI plus animatronics. It's where his monster-as-solution idea comes from, an idea he ports onto the T-Rex as the consequence of what humans did wrong.

films.title: Raiders of the Lost Ark
films.note: Rewatched as a reference for Spielberg's blend of adventure and visual comedy.

films.title: Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom
films.note: Also rewatched as a reference point.

films.title: King Kong
films.note: Bayona revisited the original to study scale, big against small. The Indoraptor's introduction carries a King Kong element.

films.title: Ready Player One
films.note: Cited as the kind of big, original fantasy that also talks about the world we live in, which is what Bayona wants to make.

films.title: The Post
films.note: Spielberg's other recent film, held up as the small, meaningful counterpart to the blockbuster.

films.title: Hereditary
films.note: Bayona's last great thing, seen two days before taping. He calls it fascinating and uncomfortable, close to the cinema of David Lynch.

Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom.title: Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom
